_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed'; use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Rename two of the gate's three verdict labels for clarity, wording only — behavior is byte-for-byte identical. ESCALATE→REVIEW (a human signs off; 'escalate' alarmed users, sounding like an incident, while 'review' is a normal step) and REVISE→REWORK (the agent fixes it and re-pushes; 'revise'/'review' looked too alike, so revise→rework keeps the two distinct). APPROVE is unchanged. The Verdict type, the review prompt's allowed values + the parseReviewJson validation list, the confidence/self_modifying downgrade targets, the PR-comment headers (plumbline: REVIEW / plumbline: REWORK), the CI-annotation titles/levels, the failure-capsule severity value ('escalation'→'review'), and all prose in docs/README/MISSION/AGENTS are renamed consistently. Exit codes, gating logic, the --admin override-merge instruction, ci-evidence checks, self_modifying handling, and the poll-wait are untouched.

validation_plan.reason: The Verdict union type changed; type-check proves every match arm, comparison, and test fixture uses the new labels and nothing dangles on the old ones.

Full suite (101 tests) — asserts the rendered PR-comment headers, CI-annotation titles/levels, attempt-history archive labels, and parseReviewJson verdicts under the new wording; proves behavior is unchanged.

Manual behavioral diff to confirm ONLY string literals/identifiers changed — exit-code logic (approve-only pass), gating conditions, the --admin override instruction, ci-evidence, and self_modifying handling are structurally identical.

execution_evidence.output_ref: # tests 101 # pass 101 # fail 0 (also the CI 'test' job on this PR — ci_evidence_checks corroborates)
diff is rename-only: verdict string literals + one comment/prose word per site; no control flow, condition, exit code, or override-instruction changed

result_summary: Renamed the two ambiguous verdict labels across source, machine-readable output, PR-comment/CI strings, tests, and docs: ESCALATE→REVIEW and REVISE→REWORK (APPROVE unchanged). Verified by tsc + the full 101-test suite (all green) and a manual src/ diff confirming behavior/exit-codes are identical — only human-facing wording changed. This PR touches protected surfaces (src/types.ts, src/github.ts, src/severity.ts, .plumbline/**) and changes the gate itself, so self_modifying: true — it correctly self-gates to REVIEW for human sign-off.
